differences.description: Document title changed from "The RISC-V Instruction Set Manual: Volume II" to "The RISC-V Instruction Set Manual, Volume II", and the version/release line changed from "Version 20250508: This document is in Ratified state." to "Version 20260120: Official Release".

The contributors sentence ending was corrected from "Reinoud Zandijk.." to "Reinoud Zandijk.", and the footer document title changed from "The RISC-V Instruction Set Manual: Volume II" to "The RISC-V Instruction Set Manual, Volume II".

Added a new current preface for Version 20260120 stating that the listed RISC-V ISA module versions have all been ratified; added "Svrsw60t59b Extension" version 1.0 to the module list; and added a change note since version 20250508: "Addition of the Svrsw60t59b extension for additional PTE reserved-for-software bits."

The Version 20250508 preface was converted from the current preface to a historical preface. In its module list, "Smctr", "Svvptc", "Ssqosid", and "Pointer Masking" were renamed to "Smctr Extension", "Svvptc Extension", "Ssqosid Extension", and "Pointer-Masking Extensions". In the change list since 20241101, the Ssqosid and Pointer-Masking bullets now end with periods, and the Pointer Masking entry was changed to "Addition of the Pointer-Masking Extensions."
The compatible-change bullet about synchronization requirements was expanded from "changing the PBMTE fields in menvcfg and henvcfg" to "changing the PBMTE and ADUE fields in menvcfg and henvcfg".
Added compatible-change bullet: "Defined the format of the memory-mapped msip registers."
The compatible-change bullet changed from "Defined optional mseccfg and mseccfgh CSRs" to "Defined mseccfg and mseccfgh CSRs", removing the statement that they are optional.
The reference changed from "The separate debug specification proposal describes" to "The separate debug specification describes", removing "proposal".

The explanation of CSR address bits csr[9:8] was expanded to state that pattern 10 represents hypervisor CSRs, and the virtual-instruction exception cross-reference changed from a section-number reference to a named "Trap Cause Codes" reference.

The CSR listing introduction changed from "Table 4-Table 8" to "Table 4-Table 7" for allocated CSR addresses, because the machine-level CSR listing was consolidated and indirect CSR mappings are now listed separately.

differences.section: 2.2.2. currently allocated risc-v supervisor-level csr addresses
differences.description: Added the new supervisor-level CSR subsection/table entries, including Supervisor Indirect CSRs siselect (0x150), sireg (0x151), sireg2 (0x152), sireg3 (0x153), sireg4 (0x155), sireg5 (0x156), and sireg6 (0x157); Supervisor Timer Compare CSRs stimecmp (0x14D) and stimecmph (0x15D); Supervisor Resource Management Configuration CSR srmcfg (0x181); and Supervisor Control Transfer Records CSRs sctrctl (0x14E), sctrstatus (0x14F), and sctrdepth (0x15F).

differences.section: 2.2.3. currently allocated risc-v hypervisor and vs csr addresses
differences.description: Added the hypervisor and VS CSR subsection/table entries for Virtual Supervisor Indirect CSRs: vsiselect (0x250), vsireg (0x251), vsireg2 (0x252), vsireg3 (0x253), vsireg4 (0x255), vsireg5 (0x256), and vsireg6 (0x257).

differences.section: 2.2.3. currently allocated risc-v hypervisor and vs csr addresses
differences.description: Added Virtual Supervisor Timer Compare CSRs vstimecmp (0x24D) and vstimecmph (0x25D, RV32 only), and the Virtual Supervisor Control Transfer Records CSR vsctrctl (0x24E), to the hypervisor/VS CSR listing.

differences.section: 2.2.4. currently allocated risc-v machine-level csr addresses
differences.description: Added the machine-level CSR subsection/table entries for Machine Indirect CSRs: miselect (0x350), mireg (0x351), mireg2 (0x352), mireg3 (0x353), mireg4 (0x355), mireg5 (0x356), and mireg6 (0x357).

differences.section: 2.2.4. currently allocated risc-v machine-level csr addresses
differences.description: Added machine counter-configuration CSRs mcyclecfg (0x321), minstretcfg (0x322), mcyclecfgh (0x721, RV32 only), and minstretcfgh (0x722, RV32 only) to the machine CSR listing.

differences.section: 2.2.4. currently allocated risc-v machine-level csr addresses
differences.description: Added the Machine Control Transfer Records Control Register mctrctl at CSR address 0x34E to the machine CSR listing.

differences.section: 2.2.5. currently allocated risc-v indirect csr (smcsrind) mappings
differences.description: Added a new subsection with indirect CSR mapping tables for M-mode, S-mode, and VS-mode select values, including M-mode AIA mappings such as iprio0-iprio15, eidelivery, eithreshold, eip0-eip63, and eie0-eie63; S-mode delegated counter mappings for siselect values 0x40-0x5F; and CTR entry mappings.
Commentary changed "the repo around which development occurs" to "the project around which development occurs".
The mhartid requirement changed from "at least one hart must have a hart ID of zero" to "one hart must have a hart ID of zero".

differences.section: 3.1.6.2. double trap control in mstatus register
differences.description: Added that whether performance counters and timers are updated in the critical-error state is UNSPECIFIED.

The MPRV description was broadened from modifying the effective privilege mode for loads and stores to modifying it for explicit memory accesses; the MPRV=0 behavior likewise now says explicit memory accesses behave as normal.
The TW field wording now refers to WFI execution and exceptions in "modes less privileged than M" instead of "lower privilege modes" or "less-privileged modes".

The U-mode WFI rule now states that when S-mode is implemented, executing WFI in U-mode causes an illegal-instruction exception regardless of TW unless it completes within the bounded time limit; the prior future-revision note about selectively permitting WFI in U-mode when TW=0 was removed.

The MSIP description changed from saying memory-mapped control registers are used by remote harts and that a hart can write its own MSIP bit through the same register, to saying MSIP is written by accesses to memory-mapped control registers used to provide machine-level interprocessor interrupts.

The STIP/STIE description now incorporates Sstc behavior: if stimecmp is implemented, STIP is read-only in mip and reflects the supervisor timer interrupt signal from stimecmp; if stimecmp is not implemented, STIP remains writable for M-mode software timer interrupt injection.
The RV32 counter access paragraph now contains the typo "return bitj 31-0" instead of "return bits 31-0".

Added Sstc-related mcounteren.TM behavior: clearing TM causes accesses to stimecmp or vstimecmp from modes less privileged than M to raise illegal-instruction exceptions, while setting TM permits S-mode access and permits VS-mode access to vstimecmp via stimecmp subject to hcounteren.TM and implementation rules.

The mtval faulting-address rules were expanded to include hardware-error exceptions, including for instruction fetch, load, or store cases and for misaligned load/store and variable-length instruction faults in addition to access-fault and page-fault exceptions.

Added a rule that on a breakpoint exception raised by EBREAK or C.EBREAK, mtval is written with either zero or the virtual address of the instruction, with commentary explaining that recording the EBREAK instruction address is redundant with mepc but provided for backwards compatibility.
The mconfigptr alignment formula was clarified: if MXLEN is 8×n, then mconfigptr[log2 n - 1:0] must be zero.

Added an ADUE synchronization rule requiring SFENCE.VMA with rs1=x0 and rs2=x0 after changing menvcfg.ADUE to synchronize address-translation caches with the altered interpretation of PTE A/D bits, with additional hypervisor requirements referenced. The section also adds direct detailed definitions for Zicboz/Zicbom CBZE, CBCFE, and CBIE behavior, including enable behavior, illegal-instruction behavior, and CBIE encodings.

Several menvcfg field definitions were changed from short references to their furnishing extensions into inline definitions: STCE is fully defined as the Sstc stimecmp enable bit with access, read-only-zero, henvcfg.STCE, and STIP/VSTIP fallback behavior; CBZE, CBCFE, and CBIE are defined for Zicboz/Zicbom cache-block instruction enable and CBIE encoding behavior; and PMM is defined with legal values for pointer masking and read-only-zero behavior when Smnpm is absent or on RV32.
mseccfg changed from "an optional 64-bit read/write register" to a 64-bit read/write register that exists if any extension adding a field to mseccfg is implemented; otherwise it is reserved.

Added detailed Zkr definitions for SSEED and USEED controlling seed CSR access from modes less privileged than M, including U-mode, S/HS-mode, and VS/VU-mode behavior and an entropy source access-control table.
Added an Entropy Source Access Control table specifying seed CSR accessibility and exception behavior in M, U, S/HS, and VS/VU modes under SSEED and USEED settings.

The RLB, MMWP, and MML fields are now defined directly in the mseccfg section rather than merely referenced as furnished by Smepmp, including sticky behavior, locked-PMP modification rules, machine-mode allowlist policy, and Machine Mode Lockdown rule changes; SSEED and USEED are also specified directly as Zkr seed CSR access-control fields rather than only referenced as furnished by Zkr.

The mseccfg.PMM field is now fully defined in the mseccfg section with legal values for M-mode pointer masking under Smmpm and read-only-zero behavior when Smmpm is not implemented or on RV32, instead of merely saying the definition is furnished by Smmpm.
The mseccfgh existence rule was clarified to state that mseccfgh exists when XLEN=32 and mseccfg is implemented, and does not exist when XLEN=64.

Added reset behavior for mseccfg fields: MML, MMWP, and RLB reset to 0 unless the platform mandates different reset values, and USEED and SSEED must have defined reset values and must not be left undefined after reset.
The AMOArithmetic definition was clarified from supporting "all RISC-V AMOs" to supporting "all RISC-V AMOs defined by the A extension".

Added Zacas PMA support levels AMOCASW, AMOCASD, and AMOCASQ for AMOCAS.W, AMOCAS.D, and AMOCAS.Q, with a note that they require AMOArithmetic support because they require arithmetic comparison and swap capability; also added that Zabha AMOs require the same PMA support level as the corresponding A-extension or Zacas instructions.
The set of instructions covered by the misaligned atomicity granule PMA was expanded to explicitly include compressed encodings of the listed AMOs, base loads/stores, and floating-point loads/stores.
PMP permission-fault rules now explicitly include cache-block management instructions as load-like/read-permission accesses and cache-block zero instructions as store-like/write-permission accesses.

Added a note explaining that address 2^(XLEN+2) cannot be represented as the top of a TOR range, so an RV32 system with 34-bit physical addresses cannot use TOR PMP to grant less-privileged modes access to the uppermost word of memory.
Removed the statement that if the current XLEN is greater than MXLEN, PMP address registers are zero-extended from MXLEN to XLEN for address matching.
The L-bit permission text now says R/W/X permissions are "additionally" enforced on M-mode accesses when L is set.

PMP priority, matching, and exception behavior was rewritten in terms of individual "memory operations" rather than accesses as a whole, clarifying behavior when an instruction decomposes into multiple memory operations, such as misaligned loads/stores, instruction fetches, or stores wider than XLEN; the related discussion was moved to the start of the subsection and tied to RVWMO memory-operation decomposition.

The RV32 state-enable CSR wording changed from saying the listed registers are 32-bit with corresponding high-half CSRs to saying there are CSR addresses for accessing the upper 32 bits of corresponding machine-level and hypervisor CSRs.
The SRMCFG bit description now says it controls access to the srmcfg CSR introduced by the Ssqosid extension, removing the old chapter-number reference.

The former Section 6.1 "Introduction" heading was removed so "Threat model" is now Section 6.1 instead of 6.1.1; the text now explicitly frames the absence of protections as "Without the Smepmp extension" and describes attacks as mitigated through the virtual-memory system rather than through SMAP/SMEP terminology.
Added a sentence pointing readers to Appendix A.1 for the rationale that guided development of the Smepmp extension.
The mitigation wording changed from attacks mitigated through "SMAP/SMEP" to attacks mitigated through "the virtual-memory system".

Removed the paragraph listing CSR numbers 0x321, 0x322, 0x721, and 0x722 for mcyclecfg, minstretcfg, mcyclecfgh, and minstretcfgh from this section; those allocations now appear in the central CSR listing.

The text changed from "When the Smcdeleg/Ssccfg extension is enabled" to "When the Smcdeleg/Ssccfg extensions are enabled". The note that hpmeventi and cyclecfg/instretcfg represent subsets of machine-level state was expanded into normative text after the indirect HPM mapping table, including that MINH is read-only zero through sireg* when Sscofpmf or Smcntrpmf is implemented.
The note identifying Smaia/Ssaia-defined registers now includes mvip along with mvien and hvien; the first document mentioned only mvien and hvien.
Commentary describing ctrdata.TYPE changed from "Transfer Type Filtering bits" to "Transfer Type Filter bits".

Added Sstc behavior for hcounteren.TM: if clear, VS-mode access to vstimecmp via stimecmp causes a virtual-instruction exception when mcounteren.TM is set; if both hcounteren.TM and mcounteren.TM are set, VS-mode access to vstimecmp is permitted if implemented.

differences.section: 15.2.18. virtual supervisor address translation and protection (vsatp) register
differences.description: The note about HLV, HLVX, or HSV instructions and VS-stage A-bit updates changed from "misspeculatively executed" virtual-machine load/store instructions to "mispredicted" virtual-machine load/store instructions.

Synchronization requirements were expanded from PBMTE-only changes to PBMTE or ADUE changes in menvcfg and henvcfg: HFENCE.GVMA synchronizes altered interpretation of G-stage and VS-stage PTE PBMT and A/D fields for menvcfg changes, and HFENCE.VVMA synchronizes altered interpretation of VS-stage PTE PBMT and A/D fields for henvcfg changes. The world-switch commentary now refers to henvcfg.PBMTE/ADUE rather than only henvcfg.PBMTE.

Added a hypervisor section describing interaction between pointer masking and two-stage address translation, including that GPAs are two bits wider than corresponding virtual address modes, behavior when VS/VU runs with vsatp.MODE=Bare, use of senvcfg.PMM/henvcfg.PMM and hgatp.MODE, cases requiring HFENCE.GVMA with rs1=x0 when henvcfg.PMM changes, and guidance for address-specific HFENCE.GVMA implementations to ignore the address argument or top masked GPA bits when matching.
In the hypervisor trap-cause table, interrupt exception code 13 changed from "Reserved for counter-overflow interrupt" to "Counter-overflow interrupt".

differences.section: 18.2.6. memory accesses subject to pointer masking
differences.description: Hardware CSR write and debug address-trigger matching text now qualifies pointer masking with "when applicable"; the stval example now says hardware applies pointer masking to an address written into stval only if pointer masking is applicable to that address.
Added a "Control Transfer Records Management Instructions" category containing the SCTRCLR instruction encoding 0001000 00100 00000 000 00000 1110011.
The Bibliography is no longer a standalone final section; in the second document the bibliography entries appear appended at the end of Appendix A.1 after "== Bibliography".

The current release module table changed from a three-column Module/Version/Status table, with every status shown as Ratified, to a two-column Module/Version table preceded by text stating all modules are ratified. Module names were normalized, including "Smctr" to "Smctr Extension", "Svvptc" to "Svvptc Extension", and "Pointer Masking" to "Pointer-Masking Extensions".
Added that for a not-taken branch, ctrtarget holds the PC of the next sequential instruction following the branch.

PTE page-permission fault rules now explicitly include cache-block management instructions as load-like accesses requiring read permission and cache-block zero instructions as store-like accesses requiring write permission.

Step 9 now specifies that the PMA/PMP check is for a store to the PTE at address a+va.vpn[i]×PTESIZE rather than a generic store to pte, and explicitly says to store the updated pte back to that PTE address after setting A and optionally D.
The Sv48 leaf page sizes are now explicitly listed as 4 KiB pages, 2 MiB megapages, 1 GiB gigapages, and 512 GiB terapages instead of the generic "pages, megapages, gigapages, and terapages" wording.

The Sv57 leaf page sizes are now explicitly listed as 4 KiB pages, 2 MiB megapages, 1 GiB gigapages, 512 GiB terapages, and 256 TiB petapages instead of the generic "pages, megapages, gigapages, terapages, and petapages" wording.
